finding: Proportion congruent effect is driven by implicit contingency learning, not strategic attention modulation. High contingency trials speed responses, low contingency trials do not slow responses; effect is independent of Stroop congruency and operates across facilitation only in latencies but facilitation plus interference in errors.

General action versus inaction primes increase or decrease motor and cognitive output across diverse behaviors. Effect is goal-mediated (satisfied by appropriate activity) not merely concept-driven; activation precedes satisfaction and reverses when goal is achieved through opposing immediate behavior.

Perceptions of moral agency and patiency are inversely related. Agents perceived as less vulnerable; patients perceived as less responsible. Inverse relation is independent of valence, causal bidirectional, and extends to neutral targets paired with agents or patients (phantom dyad).

Liberals ground morality primarily in two individualizing foundations (harm and fairness); conservatives weight five moral foundations more equally including binding foundations (ingroup, authority, purity). Pattern replicates across explicit judgments, implicit associations, and spontaneous language use in religious texts.

Disgust sensitivity predicts intuitive disapproval of homosexuality independent of explicit moral judgment. High disgust sensitivity shows stronger automatic negative associations and higher intentionality attributions for gay kissing, but explicit wrongness judgments are uncorrelated with disgust.

Visual perspective (third-person versus first-person) causally links to action identification abstractness bidirectionally. Third-person viewing increases abstract interpretation (why/goals); abstract descriptions increase preference for third-person images. Relationship holds for own actions and others' actions across mental and photographic imagery.
